text.KJV: And said, O my God, I am ashamed and blush to lift up my face to thee, my God: for our iniquities are increased over our head, and our trespass is grown up unto the heavens.
text.NABRE: A Penitential Prayer. I said: “My God, I am too ashamed and humiliated to raise my face to you, my God, for our wicked deeds are heaped up above our heads and our guilt reaches up to heaven.
text.DRB: and said: My God I am confounded and ashamed to lift up my face to thee: because our iniquities are multiplied over our head, and our sins have grown even unto heaven.

text.NRSV: From the days of our ancestors to this day we have been deep in guilt, and for our iniquities we, our kings, and our priests have been handed over to the kings of the lands, to the sword, to captivity, to plundering, and to utter shame, as is now the case.
text.Rotherham: Since the days of our fathers, we, have been in great guilt, until this day,—and, for our iniquities, have we been given up—we, our kings, our priests,—into the hand of the kings of the lands, by sword and by captivity and by spoiling and by a turning pale of face, as at this day.
text.KJV: Since the days of our fathers have we been in a great trespass unto this day; and for our iniquities have we, our kings, and our priests, been delivered into the hand of the kings of the lands, to the sword, to captivity, and to a spoil, and to confusion of face, as it is this day.
text.NABRE: From the time of our ancestors even to this day our guilt has been great, and for our wicked deeds we have been delivered, we and our kings and our priests, into the hands of the kings of foreign lands, to the sword, to captivity, to pillage, and to disgrace, as is the case today.
text.DRB: from the days of our fathers: yea and we ourselves also have sinned grievously unto this day, and in our iniquities have we been delivered, ourselves, and our kings, and our Priests, into the hand of the kings of the lands, and into the sword, and into captivity, and into spoil, and into confusion of countenance, as also at this day.

text.NRSV: But now for a brief moment favor has been shown by the Lord our God, who has left us a remnant, and given us a stake in his holy place, in order that he may brighten our eyes and grant us a little sustenance in our slavery.
text.Rotherham: And, now, for a very little moment, hath come favour from Yahweh our God, in leaving to us a remnant to escape, and in giving to us a nail in his holy place,—that our God may enlighten our eyes, and give us a little reviving in our bondage.
text.KJV: And now for a little space grace hath been shewed from the Lord our God, to leave us a remnant to escape, and to give us a nail in his holy place, that our God may lighten our eyes, and give us a little reviving in our bondage.
text.NABRE: “And now, only a short time ago, mercy came to us from the Lord, our God, who left us a remnant and gave us a stake in his holy place; thus our God has brightened our eyes and given us relief in our slavery.
text.DRB: And now as it were a little, and for a moment was our prayer made before the Lord our God, that a remnant might be left us, and a nail might be given us in his holy place, and that our God would illuminate our eyes, and would give us a little life in our bondage.

text.NRSV: For we are slaves; yet our God has not forsaken us in our slavery, but has extended to us his steadfast love before the kings of Persia, to give us new life to set up the house of our God, to repair its ruins, and to give us a wall in Judea and Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: For, bondmen, we are, but, in our bondage, hath our God not forsaken us,—but extended unto us lovingkindness before the kings of Persia, to give us a reviving, to set up on high the house of our God, to raise up the desolations thereof, and to give us a wall in Judah and in Jerusalem.
text.KJV: For we were bondmen; yet our God hath not forsaken us in our bondage, but hath extended mercy unto us in the sight of the kings of Persia, to give us a reviving, to set up the house of our God, and to repair the desolations thereof, and to give us a wall in Judah and in Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: For slaves we are, but in our slavery our God has not abandoned us; rather, he has turned the good will of the kings of Persia toward us. Thus he has given us new life to raise again the house of our God and restore its ruins, and has granted us a protective wall in Judah and Jerusalem.
text.DRB: Because we are bondmen, and in our bondage our God did not forsake us, but he inclined mercy upon us before the king of the Persians, to give us life, & to advance the house of our God, & to build the desolations thereof, & to give us a hedge in Juda & Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: “And now, our God, what shall we say after this? For we have forsaken your commandments,
text.Rotherham: But, now, what can we say, O our God, after this? for we have forsaken thy commandments,
text.KJV: And now, O our God, what shall we say after this? for we have forsaken thy commandments,
text.NABRE: But now, our God, what can we say after all this? For we have abandoned your commandments,
text.DRB: And now what shall we say, o our God, after these things? Because we have forsaken thy commandments,

text.NRSV: which you commanded by your servants the prophets, saying, ‘The land that you are entering to possess is a land unclean with the pollutions of the peoples of the lands, with their abominations. They have filled it from end to end with their uncleanness.
text.Rotherham: which thou didst command by the hand of thy servants the prophets, saying, As for the land which, ye, are entering to possess, an impure land, it is, with the impurity of the peoples of the lands,—with their abominations, which have filled it from one end to the other, with their uncleanness.
text.KJV: Which thou hast commanded by thy servants the prophets, saying, The land, unto which ye go to possess it, is an unclean land with the filthiness of the people of the lands, with their abominations, which have filled it from one end to another with their uncleanness.
text.NABRE: which you gave through your servants the prophets: The land which you are entering to take as your possession is a land unclean with the filth of the peoples of the lands, with the abominations with which they have filled it from one end to the other by their uncleanness.
text.DRB: which thou hast commanded in the hand of thy servants the Prophets, saying: The land, to the which you enter to possess it, is an unclean land, according to the uncleanness of peoples, and of other lands, by the abominations of them that have filled it from mouth unto mouth in their coinquination.

text.NRSV: Therefore do not give your daughters to their sons, neither take their daughters for your sons, and never seek their peace or prosperity, so that you may be strong and eat the good of the land and leave it for an inheritance to your children forever.’
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, your daughters, do not ye give to their sons, and, their daughters, do not ye take for your sons, neither shall ye seek their prosperity nor their pleasure unto times age-abiding,—to the end ye may become strong, and may eat the good of the land, and may suffer your children to possess it, unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: Now therefore give not your daughters unto their sons, neither take their daughters unto your sons, nor seek their peace or their wealth for ever: that ye may be strong, and eat the good of the land, and leave it for an inheritance to your children for ever.
text.NABRE: Do not, then, give your daughters to their sons in marriage, and do not take their daughters for your sons. Never promote their welfare and prosperity; thus you will grow strong, enjoy the produce of the land, and leave it as an inheritance to your children forever.
text.DRB: Now therefore give not your daughters to their sons, and their daughters take not for your sons, and do ye not seek their peace, and their prosperity for ever: that you may be strengthened, and may eat the goods that are of the land, and may have your children heirs for ever.

text.NRSV: After all that has come upon us for our evil deeds and for our great guilt, seeing that you, our God, have punished us less than our iniquities deserved and have given us such a remnant as this,
text.Rotherham: And, after all that hath come upon us, for our wicked doings, and for our great guilt—For, thou, O our God, hast spared us, punishing us less than our iniquities deserved, and hast given us a deliverance such as this,
text.KJV: And after all that is come upon us for our evil deeds, and for our great trespass, seeing that thou our God hast punished us less than our iniquities deserve, and hast given us such deliverance as this;
text.NABRE: “After all that has come upon us for our evil deeds and our great guilt—though you, our God, have made less of our sinfulness than it deserved and have allowed us to survive as we do—
text.DRB: And after all things that come upon us in our most wicked works, and our most great sin, because thou our God hast delivered us from our iniquity, and hast given us health as it is at this day,

text.NRSV: shall we break your commandments again and intermarry with the peoples who practice these abominations? Would you not be angry with us until you destroy us without remnant or survivor?
text.Rotherham: should we again break thy commandments and join ourselves by affinity of marriage with the peoples of these abominations,—wouldst thou not be angry with us, unto a full end, that there should be neither remainder nor deliverance?
text.KJV: Should we again break thy commandments, and join in affinity with the people of these abominations? wouldest not thou be angry with us till thou hadst consumed us, so that there should be no remnant nor escaping?
text.NABRE: shall we again violate your commandments by intermarrying with these abominable peoples? Would you not become so angered with us as to destroy us without remnant or survivor?
text.DRB: that we shall not turn away, & make frustrate thy commandments, neither should join matrimonies with the peoples of these abominations. Why art thou angry with us unto utter destruction, not to leave us a remnant unto salvation?

text.NRSV: O Lord, God of Israel, you are just, but we have escaped as a remnant, as is now the case. Here we are before you in our guilt, though no one can face you because of this.”
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh, God of Israel, righteous thou art, for we have had left us a deliverance as at this day,—here we are, before thee, in our guilty deeds, for there is no standing before thee, because of this thing!
text.KJV: O Lord God of Israel, thou art righteous: for we remain yet escaped, as it is this day: behold, we are before thee in our trespasses: for we cannot stand before thee because of this.
text.NABRE: Lord, God of Israel, you are just; yet we have been spared, the remnant we are today. Here we are before you in our sins. Because of all this, we can no longer stand in your presence.”
text.DRB: Lord God of Israel thou art just: because we are left, which should be saved as at this day. Behold we are before thee in our sin, for there can be no standing before thee upon this.

text.NRSV: While Ezra prayed and made confession, weeping and throwing himself down before the house of God, a very great assembly of men, women, and children gathered to him out of Israel; the people also wept bitterly.
text.Rotherham: Now, when Ezra had prayed and when he had made confession, weeping and casting himself down, before the house of God, there gathered unto him out of Israel, an exceeding large convocation—men and women and children, for the people wept with a very bitter weeping.
text.KJV: Now when Ezra had prayed, and when he had confessed, weeping and casting himself down before the house of God, there assembled unto him out of Israel a very great congregation of men and women and children: for the people wept very sore.
text.NABRE: While Ezra prayed and acknowledged their guilt, weeping and prostrate before the house of God, a very large assembly of Israelites gathered about him, men, women, and children; and the people wept profusely.
text.DRB: Esdras therefore thus praying, and beseeching, and weeping, and lying before the temple of God, there was gathered to him of Israel an exceeding great company of men and women and children, and the people wept with much lamentation.

text.NRSV: Shecaniah son of Jehiel, of the descendants of Elam, addressed Ezra, saying, “We have broken faith with our God and have married foreign women from the peoples of the land, but even now there is hope for Israel in spite of this.
text.Rotherham: Then responded Shecaniah son of Jehiel of the sons of Elam, and said unto Ezra, We, have been faithful with our God, and have married foreign women from among the peoples of the land,—yet, now, there is hope for Israel concerning this thing.
text.KJV: And Shechaniah the son of Jehiel, one of the sons of Elam, answered and said unto Ezra, We have trespassed against our God, and have taken strange wives of the people of the land: yet now there is hope in Israel concerning this thing.
text.NABRE: Then Shecaniah, the son of Jehiel, one of the descendants of Elam, made this appeal to Ezra: “We have indeed betrayed our God by taking as wives foreign women of the peoples of the land. Yet in spite of this there still remains a hope for Israel.
text.DRB: And Sechenias the son of Jehiel of the children of Ælam answered, and said to Esdras: We have transgressed against our God, and have taken to wives strange women of the peoples of the land: and now if there be penance in Israel upon this,

text.NRSV: So now let us make a covenant with our God to send away all these wives and their children, according to the counsel of my lord and of those who tremble at the commandment of our God; and let it be done according to the law.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, let us solemnize a covenant unto our God—to put away all the women and such as have been born of them, in the counsel of my lord, and them who tremble at the commandment of our God,—and, according to the law, let it be done.
text.KJV: Now therefore let us make a covenant with our God to put away all the wives, and such as are born of them, according to the counsel of my lord, and of those that tremble at the commandment of our God; and let it be done according to the law.
text.NABRE: Let us therefore enter into a covenant before our God to dismiss all our foreign wives and the children born of them, in keeping with what you, my lord, advise, and those who are in dread of the commandments of our God. Let it be done according to the law!
text.DRB: let us make a covenant with the Lord our God, to put away all the wives, and them that are born of them, according to the will of our Lord, and of them that fear the precept of the Lord our God: be it done according to the law.

text.NRSV: Take action, for it is your duty, and we are with you; be strong, and do it.”
text.Rotherham: Arise, for, upon thee, resteth the thing, but, we, are with thee,—be strong, and act.
text.KJV: Arise; for this matter belongeth unto thee: we also will be with thee: be of good courage, and do it.
text.NABRE: Rise, then, for this is your duty! We are with you, so have courage and act!”
text.DRB: Arise, it is thy part to discern, and we will be with thee: take courage, and do it.

text.NRSV: Then Ezra stood up and made the leading priests, the Levites, and all Israel swear that they would do as had been said. So they swore.
text.Rotherham: Then arose Ezra and made the rulers of the priests, the Levites, and all Israel, swear to do according to this word—and they sware.
text.KJV: Then arose Ezra, and made the chief priests, the Levites, and all Israel, to swear that they should do according to this word. And they sware.
text.NABRE: Ezra stood and demanded an oath from the leaders of the priests, from the Levites and from all Israel that they would do as had been proposed; and they swore it.
text.DRB: Esdras therefore rose up, and adjured the Princes of the Priests and of the Levites, & all Israel, that they should do according to this word, and they sware.

text.NRSV: Then Ezra withdrew from before the house of God, and went to the chamber of Jehohanan son of Eliashib, where he spent the night. He did not eat bread or drink water, for he was mourning over the faithlessness of the exiles.
text.Rotherham: Then arose Ezra from before the house of God, and went into the chamber of Jehohanan son of Eliashib,—and, when he came thither, bread, did he not eat, and, water, did he not drink, for he was mourning over the unfaithfulness of them of the Exile.
text.KJV: Then Ezra rose up from before the house of God, and went into the chamber of Johanan the son of Eliashib: and when he came thither, he did eat no bread, nor drink water: for he mourned because of the transgression of them that had been carried away.
text.NABRE: Then Ezra left his place before the house of God and entered the chamber of Johanan, son of Eliashib, where he spent the night neither eating food nor drinking water, for he was in mourning over the apostasy of the exiles.
text.DRB: And Esdras rose up before the house of God, & went to the chamber of Johanan the son of Eliasib, and entered into it: he did eat no bread, & drank no water: for he mourned for the transgression of them that were come out of the captivity.

text.NRSV: They made a proclamation throughout Judah and Jerusalem to all the returned exiles that they should assemble at Jerusalem,
text.Rotherham: Then made they a proclamation throughout Judah and Jerusalem, unto all the Sons of the Exile, to gather themselves together unto Jerusalem;
text.KJV: And they made proclamation throughout Judah and Jerusalem unto all the children of the captivity, that they should gather themselves together unto Jerusalem;
text.NABRE: A proclamation was made throughout Judah and Jerusalem that all the exiles should gather together in Jerusalem,
text.DRB: And there was a proclamation sent in Juda and Jerusalem to all the children of the transgression, that they should assemble together into Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: and that if any did not come within three days, by order of the officials and the elders all their property should be forfeited, and they themselves banned from the congregation of the exiles.
text.Rotherham: and, whosoever should not come within three days, according to the counsel of the rulers and the elders, all his goods should be devoted,—and, himself, be separated from the convocation of them of the Exile.
text.KJV: And that whosoever would not come within three days, according to the counsel of the princes and the elders, all his substance should be forfeited, and himself separated from the congregation of those that had been carried away.
text.NABRE: and that whoever failed to appear within three days would, according to the judgment of the leaders and elders, suffer the confiscation of all his possessions, and would be excluded from the assembly of the exiles.
text.DRB: And every one that shall not come within three days, according to the counsel of the Princes & ancients, all his substance shall be taken away and himself shall be cast out of the company of the transmigration.

text.NRSV: Then all the people of Judah and Benjamin assembled at Jerusalem within the three days; it was the ninth month, on the twentieth day of the month. All the people sat in the open square before the house of God, trembling because of this matter and because of the heavy rain.
text.Rotherham: Then were gathered together all the men of Judah and Benjamin unto Jerusalem, within three days, the same, was the ninth month, on the twentieth of the month,—and all the people remained in the broadway of the house of God, trembling concerning the thing, and because of the heavy rains.
text.KJV: Then all the men of Judah and Benjamin gathered themselves together unto Jerusalem within three days. It was the ninth month, on the twentieth day of the month; and all the people sat in the street of the house of God, trembling because of this matter, and for the great rain.
text.NABRE: All the men of Judah and Benjamin gathered together in Jerusalem within the three-day period: it was in the ninth month, on the twentieth day of the month. All the people, sitting in the open place before the house of God, were trembling both over the matter at hand and because it was raining.
text.DRB: There assembled therefore all the men of Juda, and Benjamin into Jerusalem within three days, that is the ninth month, the twentieth day of the month: and all the people sat in the street of the house of God, trembling for the sin, and the rain.

text.NRSV: Then Ezra the priest stood up and said to them, “You have trespassed and married foreign women, and so increased the guilt of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Then arose Ezra the priest, and said unto them, Ye, have acted unfaithfully, and have married foreign women,—to add unto the guilt of Israel.
text.KJV: And Ezra the priest stood up, and said unto them, Ye have transgressed, and have taken strange wives, to increase the trespass of Israel.
text.NABRE: Then Ezra, the priest, stood up and said to them: “Your apostasy in taking foreign women as wives has added to Israel’s guilt.
text.DRB: And Esdras the Priest arose, and said to them: You have transgressed, and taken strange women to wife, to add upon the sin of Israel.

text.NRSV: Now make confession to the Lord the God of your ancestors, and do his will; separate yourselves from the peoples of the land and from the foreign wives.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, make confession unto Yahweh God of your fathers, and do his pleasure,—and separate yourselves from the peoples of the land, and from the foreign women.
text.KJV: Now therefore make confession unto the Lord God of your fathers, and do his pleasure: and separate yourselves from the people of the land, and from the strange wives.
text.NABRE: But now, give praise to the Lord, the God of your ancestors, and do his will: separate yourselves from the peoples of the land and from the foreign women.”
text.DRB: And now give confession to our Lord the God of your fathers, and do his pleasure, and be separated from the peoples of the land, and from your wives the strangers.

text.NRSV: Then all the assembly answered with a loud voice, “It is so; we must do as you have said.
text.Rotherham: Then responded all the convocation and said, with a loud voice,—Thus, according to thy word concerning us, must it be done.
text.KJV: Then all the congregation answered and said with a loud voice, As thou hast said, so must we do.
text.NABRE: In answer, the whole assembly cried out with a loud voice: “Yes, it is our duty to do as you say!
text.DRB: And all the multitude answered, and said with a loud voice: According to thy word unto us, so be it done.

text.NRSV: But the people are many, and it is a time of heavy rain; we cannot stand in the open. Nor is this a task for one day or for two, for many of us have transgressed in this matter.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, the people, are many, and, the season, is that of the heavy rains, and we are not able to stand outside,—and, the business, is not one for a single day, nor yet for two, for we have abundantly transgressed, in this thing.
text.KJV: But the people are many, and it is a time of much rain, and we are not able to stand without, neither is this a work of one day or two: for we are many that have transgressed in this thing.
text.NABRE: But the people are numerous and it is the rainy season, so that we cannot remain outside; besides, this is not a task that can be performed in a single day or even two, for those of us who have sinned in this regard are many.
text.DRB: Nevertheless because there is much people, and a time of rain, and we cannot abide to stand without, and it is not a work of one day or two (for we have exceedingly sinned in this thing),

text.NRSV: Let our officials represent the whole assembly, and let all in our towns who have taken foreign wives come at appointed times, and with them the elders and judges of every town, until the fierce wrath of our God on this account is averted from us.”
text.Rotherham: Let, we beseech thee, our rulers take up their station for all the convocation, and, all who, throughout our cities, have married foreign women, let them come in at times appointed, and, with them, the elders of every city, and the judges thereof, until the glow of the anger of our God be turned from us, concerning this matter.
text.KJV: Let now our rulers of all the congregation stand, and let all them which have taken strange wives in our cities come at appointed times, and with them the elders of every city, and the judges thereof, until the fierce wrath of our God for this matter be turned from us.
text.NABRE: Let our leaders represent the whole assembly; then let all those in our cities who have taken foreign women for wives appear at appointed times, accompanied by the elders and magistrates of each city in question, till we have turned away from us our God’s burning anger over this affair.”
text.DRB: let there be Princes appointed in all the multitude: & let all in our cities that have taken strangers to wife, come at set times, and with them the ancients by city and city, and the judges thereof, until the wrath of our God be turned away from us for this sin.

text.NRSV: Only Jonathan son of Asahel and Jahzeiah son of Tikvah opposed this, and Meshullam and Shabbethai the Levites supported them.
text.Rotherham: Only Jonathan son of Asahel and Jahzeiah son of Tikvah, made a stand against this,—and, Meshullam and Shabbethai the Levite, helped them.
text.KJV: Only Jonathan the son of Asahel and Jahaziah the son of Tikvah were employed about this matter: and Meshullam and Shabbethai the Levite helped them.
text.NABRE: Only Jonathan, son of Asahel, and Jahzeiah, son of Tikvah, were against this proposal, with Meshullam and Shabbethai the Levite supporting them.
text.DRB: Therefore Jonathan the son of Azahel, and Iaazia the son of Thecua were appointed over this, and Mosollam and Sebethai Levites did help them:

text.NRSV: Then the returned exiles did so. Ezra the priest selected men, heads of families, according to their families, each of them designated by name. On the first day of the tenth month they sat down to examine the matter.
text.Rotherham: But the Sons of the Exile, did thus, and Ezra the priest separated to himself certain men—ancestral heads by their ancestral houses, and all of them by name,—and they took their seats on the first day of the tenth month to search into the matter;
text.KJV: And the children of the captivity did so. And Ezra the priest, with certain chief of the fathers, after the house of their fathers, and all of them by their names, were separated, and sat down in the first day of the tenth month to examine the matter.
text.NABRE: The exiles did as agreed. Ezra the priest appointed as his assistants men who were heads of ancestral houses, one for each ancestral house, all of them designated by name. They held sessions to examine the matter, beginning with the first day of the tenth month.
text.DRB: and the children of the transmigration did so. And Esdras the Priest, and the men, Princes of the families, went into the houses of their fathers, & all by their names, and they sat in the first day of the tenth month to search out the matter.

text.NRSV: By the first day of the first month they had come to the end of all the men who had married foreign women.
text.Rotherham: and they made an end with all the men who had married foreign women,—by the first day of the first month.
text.KJV: And they made an end with all the men that had taken strange wives by the first day of the first month.
text.NABRE: By the first day of the first month they had finished dealing with all the men who had taken foreign women for wives.
text.DRB: And all the men were fully counted that had taken strangers to wife, unto the first day of the first month.

text.NRSV: There were found of the descendants of the priests who had married foreign women, of the descendants of Jeshua son of Jozadak and his brothers: Maaseiah, Eliezer, Jarib, and Gedaliah.
text.Rotherham: Now there were found, of the sons of the priests, who had married foreign women,—of the sons of Jeshua son of Jozadak, and his brethren, Maaseiah, and Eliezer, and Jarib, and Gedaliah;
text.KJV: And among the sons of the priests there were found that had taken strange wives: namely, of the sons of Jeshua the son of Jozadak, and his brethren; Maaseiah, and Eliezer, and Jarib, and Gedaliah.
text.NABRE: The List of Transgressors. Among the priests, the following were found to have taken foreign women for wives: Of the descendants of Jeshua, son of Jozadak, and his kinsmen: Maaseiah, Eliezer, Jarib, and Gedaliah.
text.DRB: And there were found of the sons of the Priests that had taken strangers to wife. Of the children of Josue the son of Josedec, and his brethren, Maasia, and Eliezer, and Jarib, and Godolia.

text.NRSV: They pledged themselves to send away their wives, and their guilt offering was a ram of the flock for their guilt.
text.Rotherham: and they gave their hand, that they would put away their wives,—and, being guilty, [they offered] a ram of the flock for their guilt.
text.KJV: And they gave their hands that they would put away their wives; and being guilty, they offered a ram of the flock for their trespass.
text.NABRE: They pledged themselves to dismiss their wives, and as a guilt offering for their guilt they gave a ram from the flock.
text.DRB: And they gave their hands to put away their wives, & to offer for their offence a ram of the flock.

text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Immer: Hanani and Zebadiah.
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Immer, Hanani, and Zebadiah.
text.KJV: And of the sons of Immer; Hanani, and Zebadiah.
text.NABRE: Of the descendants of Immer: Hanani and Zebadiah;
text.DRB: And of the children of Emmer, Hanani, and Zebedia.

text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Harim: Maaseiah, Elijah, Shemaiah, Jehiel, and Uzziah.
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Harim, Maaseiah and Elijah and Shemaiah, and Jehiel, and Uzziah.
text.KJV: And of the sons of Harim; Maaseiah, and Elijah, and Shemaiah, and Jehiel, and Uzziah.
text.NABRE: of the descendants of Harim: Maaseiah, Elijah, Shemaiah, Jehiel, and Uzziah;
text.DRB: And of the children of Harim, Maasia, and Elia, and Semeia, and Jehiel, and Ozias.

text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Pashhur: Elioenai, Maaseiah, Ishmael, Nethanel, Jozabad, and Elasah.
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Passhur, Elioenai, Maaseiah, Ishmael, Nethanel, Jozabad, and Elasar.
text.KJV: And of the sons of Pashur; Elioenai, Maaseiah, Ishmael, Nethaneel, Jozabad, and Elasah.
text.NABRE: of the descendants of Pashhur: Elioenai, Maaseiah, Ishmael, Nethanel, Jozabad, and Elasah.
text.DRB: And of the children of Pheshur, Elionai, Maasia, Ismael, Nathanael, Jozabed, and Elasa.

text.NRSV: Of the Levites: Jozabad, Shimei, Kelaiah (that is, Kelita), Pethahiah, Judah, and Eliezer.
text.Rotherham: And, of the Levites, Jozabad and Shimei, and Kelaiah, the same, is Kelita, Pethahiah, Judah, and Eliezer.
text.KJV: Also of the Levites; Jozabad, and Shimei, and Kelaiah, (the same is Kelita,) Pethahiah, Judah, and Eliezer.
text.NABRE: Of the Levites: Jozabad, Shimei, Kelaiah (also called Kelita), Pethahiah, Judah, and Eliezer.
text.DRB: And of the children of the Levites, Jozabed, and Semei, and Celaia, the same is Calita, Phataia, Juda, & Eliezer.

text.NRSV: Of the singers: Eliashib. Of the gatekeepers: Shallum, Telem, and Uri.
text.Rotherham: And, of the singers, Eliashib. And, of the doorkeepers, Shallum and Telem and Uri.
text.KJV: Of the singers also; Eliashib: and of the porters; Shallum, and Telem, and Uri.
text.NABRE: Of the singers: Eliashib; of the gatekeepers: Shallum, Telem, and Uri.
text.DRB: And of the singing men Eliasib: and of the porters, Sellum, and Thelem, and Uri.

text.NRSV: And of Israel: of the descendants of Parosh: Ramiah, Izziah, Malchijah, Mijamin, Eleazar, Hashabiah, and Benaiah.
text.Rotherham: And, of Israel, of the sons of Parosh, Ramiah and Izziah and Malchijah and Mijamin and Eleazar, and Malchijah, and Benaiah.
text.KJV: Moreover of Israel: of the sons of Parosh; Ramiah, and Jeziah, and Malchiah, and Miamin, and Eleazar, and Malchijah, and Benaiah.
text.NABRE: Of the people of Israel: Of the descendants of Parosh: Ramiah, Izziah, Malchijah, Mijamin, Eleazar, Malchijah, and Benaiah;
text.DRB: And of Israel, of the children of Pharos, Remeia, and Iezia, and Melchia, and Miamin, and Eliezer, and Melchia, and Banea.

text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Elam: Mattaniah, Zechariah, Jehiel, Abdi, Jeremoth, and Elijah.
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Elam, Mattaniah, Zechariah, and Jehiel and Abdi, and Jeremoth, and Elijah.
text.KJV: And of the sons of Elam; Mattaniah, Zechariah, and Jehiel, and Abdi, and Jeremoth, and Eliah.
text.NABRE: of the descendants of Elam: Mattaniah, Zechariah, Jehiel, Abdi, Jeremoth, and Elijah;
text.DRB: And of the children of Ælam, Mathania, Zacharias, and Jehiel, and Abdi, and Jerimoth, and Elia.

text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Zattu: Elioenai, Eliashib, Mattaniah, Jeremoth, Zabad, and Aziza.
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Zattu, Elioenai, Eliashib, Mattaniah and Jeremoth, and Zabad, and Aziza.
text.KJV: And of the sons of Zattu; Elioenai, Eliashib, Mattaniah, and Jeremoth, and Zabad, and Aziza.
text.NABRE: of the descendants of Zattu: Elioenai, Eliashib, Mattaniah, Jeremoth, Zabad, and Aziza;
text.DRB: And of the children of Zethua, Elioenai, Eliasib, Mathania, Jerimuth, and Zabad, and Aziza.

text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Bebai: Jehohanan, Hananiah, Zabbai, and Athlai.
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Bebai, Jehohanan, Hananiah, Zabbai, Athlai.
text.KJV: Of the sons also of Bebai; Jehohanan, Hananiah, Zabbai, and Athlai.
text.NABRE: of the descendants of Bebai: Jehohanan, Hananiah, Zabbai, and Athlai;
text.DRB: And of the children of Bebai, Johanan, Hanania, Zabbai, Athalai.

text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Bani: Meshullam, Malluch, Adaiah, Jashub, Sheal, and Jeremoth.
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Bani, Meshullam, Malluch, and Adaiah, Jashub, and Sheal, Jeremoth.
text.KJV: And of the sons of Bani; Meshullam, Malluch, and Adaiah, Jashub, and Sheal, and Ramoth.
text.NABRE: of the descendants of Bani: Meshullam, Malluch, Adaiah, Jashub, Sheal, and Jeremoth;
text.DRB: And of the children of Bani, Mosollam, and Melluch, and Adaia, Jasub, and Saal, and Ramoth.

text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Pahath-moab: Adna, Chelal, Benaiah, Maaseiah, Mattaniah, Bezalel, Binnui, and Manasseh.
text.Rotherham: And, of the sons of Pahath-moab, Adna and Chelal,—Benaiah, Maaseiah, Mattaniah, Bezalel, and Binnui, and Manasseh.
text.KJV: And of the sons of Pahath–moab; Adna, and Chelal, Benaiah, Maaseiah, Mattaniah, Bezaleel, and Binnui, and Manasseh.
text.NABRE: of the descendants of Pahath-moab: Adna, Chelal, Benaiah, Maaseiah, Mattaniah, Bezalel, Binnui, and Manasseh;
text.DRB: And of the children of Phahath Moab, Edna & Chalal, Bananias, and Maasias, Mathanias, Beseleel, Bennui, and Manasse.

text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Harim: Eliezer, Isshijah, Malchijah, Shemaiah, Shimeon,
text.Rotherham: And, [of] the sons of Harim, Eliezer, Isshijah, Malchijah, Shemaiah, Shimeon;
text.KJV: And of the sons of Harim; Eliezer, Ishijah, Malchiah, Shemaiah, Shimeon,
text.NABRE: of the descendants of Harim: Eliezer, Isshijah, Malchijah, Shemaiah, Shimeon,
text.DRB: And of the children of Herem, Eliezer, Josue, Melchias, Semeias, Simeon,

text.NRSV: Benjamin, Malluch, and Shemariah.
text.Rotherham: Benjamin, Malluch, Shemariah.
text.KJV: Benjamin, Malluch, and Shemariah.
text.NABRE: Benjamin, Malluch, Shemariah;
text.DRB: Benjamin, Maloch, Samarias.

text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Hashum: Mattenai, Mattattah, Zabad, Eliphelet, Jeremai, Manasseh, and Shimei.
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Hashum, Mattenai, Mattattah, Zabad, Eliphelet,—Jeremai, Manasseh, Shimei.
text.KJV: Of the sons of Hashum; Mattenai, Mattathah, Zabad, Eliphelet, Jeremai, Manasseh, and Shimei.
text.NABRE: of the descendants of Hashum: Mattenai, Mattattah, Zabad, Eliphelet, Jeremai, Manasseh, Shimei;
text.DRB: And of the children of Hasom, Mathanai, Mathatha, Zabad, Eliphelet, Iermai, Manasse, Semei.

text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Bani: Maadai, Amram, Uel,
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Bani, Maadai, Amram, and Uel;
text.KJV: Of the sons of Bani; Maadai, Amram, and Uel,
text.NABRE: of the descendants of Begui: Maadai, Amram, Uel,
text.DRB: Of the children of Bani, Maaddi, Amram, and Vel,

text.NRSV: Benaiah, Bedeiah, Cheluhi,
text.Rotherham: Benaiah, Bedeiah, Cheluhu;
text.KJV: Benaiah, Bedeiah, Chelluh,
text.NABRE: Benaiah, Bedeiah, Cheluhi,
text.DRB: Baneas, and Badaias, Cheliau,

text.NRSV: Vaniah, Meremoth, Eliashib,
text.Rotherham: Vaniah, Meremoth, Eliashib;
text.KJV: Vaniah, Meremoth, Eliashib,
text.NABRE: Vaniah, Meremoth, Eliashib,
text.DRB: Vania, Marimuth, and Eliasib,

text.NRSV: Mattaniah, Mattenai, and Jaasu.
text.Rotherham: Mettaniah, Mattenai, and Jaasu;
text.KJV: Mattaniah, Mattenai, and Jaasau,
text.NABRE: Mattaniah, Mattenai, and Jaasu;
text.DRB: Mathanias, and Mathanai, and Iasi,

text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Binnui: Shimei,
text.Rotherham: and Bani, and Binnui, Shimei;
text.KJV: And Bani, and Binnui, Shimei,
text.NABRE: of the descendants of Binnui: Shimei,
text.DRB: and Bani, and Bennui, Semei,

text.NRSV: Shelemiah, Nathan, Adaiah,
text.Rotherham: and Shelemiah, and Nathan, and Adaiah;
text.KJV: And Shelemiah, and Nathan, and Adaiah,
text.NABRE: Shelemiah, Nathan, and Adaiah;
text.DRB: and Salmias, and Nathan, & Adaias,

text.NRSV: Machnadebai, Shashai, Sharai,
text.Rotherham: Machnadebai, Shashai, Sharai;
text.KJV: Machnadebai, Shashai, Sharai,
text.NABRE: of the descendants of Zachai: Shashai, Sharai,
text.DRB: and Mechnedebai, Sisai, Sarai.

text.NRSV: Azarel, Shelemiah, Shemariah,
text.Rotherham: Azarel, and Shelemiah, Shemariah;
text.KJV: Azareel, and Shelemiah, Shemariah,
text.NABRE: Azarel, Shelemiah, Shemariah,
text.DRB: Ezrel, and Selemiau, Semeria,

text.NRSV: Shallum, Amariah, and Joseph.
text.Rotherham: Shallum, Amariah, Joseph.
text.KJV: Shallum, Amariah, and Joseph.
text.NABRE: Shallum, Amariah, Joseph;
text.DRB: Sellum, Amaria, Joseph.

text.NRSV: Of the descendants of Nebo: Jeiel, Mattithiah, Zabad, Zebina, Jaddai, Joel, and Benaiah.
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Nebo, Jeiel, Mattithiah, Zabad, Zebina,—Iddo, and Joel, Benaiah.
text.KJV: Of the sons of Nebo; Jeiel, Mattithiah, Zabad, Zebina, Jadau, and Joel, Benaiah.
text.NABRE: of the descendants of Nebo: Jeiel, Mattithiah, Zabad, Zebina, Jaddai, Joel, Benaiah.
text.DRB: Of the children of Nebo, Jehiel, Mathathias, Zabad, Zabina, Ieddu, and Joel, and Banaia.

text.NRSV: All these had married foreign women, and they sent them away with their children.
text.Rotherham: All these, had taken foreign women,—and had begotten of them children.
text.KJV: All these had taken strange wives: and some of them had wives by whom they had children.
text.NABRE: All these had taken foreign wives; but they sent them away, both the women and their children.
text.DRB: All these had taken strangers to wife, and there were of them that had born children.
